response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, experienced senior engineer, but the fit for this specific role is weak. His C++ exposure is limited to a ~7-month stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a university thesis project, far short of the mandatory 5–10 years of hands-on C++ on Windows. He shows no SQL Server/Oracle depth (only general SQL/MySQL/MongoDB) and no Fixed Income, derivatives, or capital-markets domain experience — RIDE Capital was consumer-facing fintech, not investment banking. His strengths lie in TypeScript/Node.js, DLang, and web platforms, which are largely irrelevant to this mandate.
strengths: Nearly 20 years of professional software development across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded systems
Proven low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (millions of HTTP requests/sec at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic)
Strong engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, DevOps tooling, and active knowledge-sharing/mentoring
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and dated (~7 months in 2014–2015), versus a mandatory 5–10 years on Windows
No Fixed Income, derivatives, or capital-markets/investment-banking domain experience
No demonstrated SQL Server or Oracle expertise; database background is MySQL/MongoDB

key_factors.factor: Strong general engineering fundamentals and seniority
